reasoning: The community prediction is currently at 5%. The resolution criteria require a credible source (like the CDC) to state that at least 70,000 people die annually from antibiotic-resistant infections in the US before the end of 2025. The most recent major CDC report from 2019 placed this number at over 35,000. During the COVID-19 pandemic, AMR deaths spiked significantly, but likely not to the level of more than doubling to 70,000 based on CDC updates (which noted a ~15% increase in 2020). Furthermore, recent global burden reports like the 2022 GRAM study estimate large global numbers, but US-specific estimates continue to sit well below 70,000. It is highly unlikely that the CDC or another credible source will update its estimate to show a doubling (to 70,000 deaths annually) within the timeframe. I predict 4%.
